real_insight: The three pairs are different registers, not the same thing felt three times. Pleasure/pain is the felt experience as Datadog moves overnight; gain/loss is the launch hitting or missing the target; victory/defeat is the competitor outcome. The instruction is to read all three accurately and arrive at each checkpoint identical. This is not stoic suppression; it is reading without identification. The engineering analog of 'pāpam' (bondage) is the calcification into burnout, blame-displacement, defensive code — the actual long-term failure mode of the engineer who lets the dashboard / target / competitor decide who they are.
objection: Could be misread as 'don't care about metrics' — but the falsifiability explicitly rejects this ('an engineer who reads the dashboard going down with no internal response has not achieved equanimity, they have achieved disengagement, which the verse rejects in its very next clause'). The verse's positive injunction is engagement, not withdrawal. Operationally specific (8/11/2 calendar checkpoints), no aphorism.
